title: BP’s Bust-Up Is Lifting the Lid on British Boardrooms
summary: It’s not too soon to start learning lessons from the dramatic removal of BP Plc chair Albert Manifold and his vigorous reaction to his ousting. The situation already highlights the central challenges of having a non-executive chair sit at the heart of UK corporate governance.

title: Oil Set for May Slide as Traders Bet on US-Iran Truce Renewal
summary: Oil dropped to a six-week low as the US and Iran tentatively agreed to extend a ceasefire by 60 days, stoking optimism the Strait of Hormuz may soon reopen.

title: Jupiter Fund Taps Europe AI Energy Boom to Beat 91% of Peers
summary: Homing in on stocks at the heart of Europe’s electrification push has helped a Jupiter Asset Management fund team outperform 91% of its peers this year.

title: US Stocks Extend Highs as Traders Await Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
summary: US stocks modestly extended all-time highs as traders waited to see whether America and Iran could finally get a peace deal across the line and restore oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Taiwan Sees AI Thrusting Exports to Fastest Growth in 50 Years
summary: Taiwan revised up its economic growth forecast for this year, predicting that roaring demand for AI would send exports to the best performance in five decades.

title: Gold Holds Above $4,500 as Iran Deal Hopes Ease Inflation Fears
summary: Gold held a gain after reports that the US and Iran had reached a preliminary deal to extend a ceasefire and work toward an agreement to end the Middle East war, easing expectations of higher for longer interest rates.

title: ECB’s Panetta States Case for Rate Hike Without Pre-Set Path
summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Fabio Panetta acknowledged the case for an interest-rate hike while urging colleagues not to flag subsequent tightening.

title: Key Japanese Aluminum Fee Set for Record as War Squeezes Supply
summary: Rio Tinto Group and South32 Ltd. have offered Japanese clients third-quarter aluminum supply at record premiums, said traders familiar with the matter, after conflict in the Middle East tightened the global market.

title: Prices Jump in France, Italy and Spain to Bolster ECB Hike Case
summary: Inflation jumped in France, Italy and Spain, backing the case for the European Central Bank to raise interest rates for the first time since 2023.

title: SpaceX Said to Cut IPO Valuation Goal
summary: SpaceX is currently targeting a valuation of at least $1.8 trillion in its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter. Bloomberg News reported in April that SpaceX was aiming for a valuation above $2 trillion. The target is settling lower after consultations with advisers and investors, the people said, asking not to be identified as the information isn’t public. Matthew Bloxham of Bloomberg Intelligence has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: PGIM Sees ECB Hike in June as French, Spanish Inflation Rises
summary: PGIM economist Katharine Neiss weighs the impact of inflation data on European Central Bank policy as consumer prices in France and Spain accelerated to the fastest level since 2024. "My base case is that the ECB will hike 25 basis points at their June meeting," Neiss says on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: War and AI Demand Break China’s Record of Falling Export Prices
summary: China’s export prices jumped at their fastest rate in three years, as a global surge in energy costs and an AI boom broke a long streak of falling prices.

title: Fuel Prices Push Kenya Inflation Sharply Higher on War Shock
summary: Kenyan inflation accelerated to a more than two-year high in May, driven by a sharp Iran-war induced increase in local fuel prices.

title: South East Water Seeks Fix After Credit Downgrade to Junk
summary: South East Water Ltd. is in talks with industry watchdog Ofwat to retain its license after its debt was downgraded to junk by Moody’s Ratings.

title: Indian Firms Face Tougher Investor Scrutiny as Rupee Hits Record Lows
summary: The rupee’s relentless slide is prompting tougher scrutiny of Indian companies as investors and analysts press executives on their foreign-exchange strategy.

title: China’s Wuxi Taclink Is Said to Mull Rare Listing in Singapore
summary: Wuxi Taclink Optoelectronics Technology Co. is considering a listing in Singapore, a potential first for any company that already trades shares in mainland China, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Indian Power Firms Snap Up Gas to Meet Night-Time Cooling Demand
summary: India’s power generation companies have quadrupled their natural gas purchases from a domestic bourse over the past couple of months to meet surging power demand from searing summer heat.

title: US Bitcoin ETFs Bleed $2.8 Billion in Longest Outflow Streak
summary: Investors pulled money from US spot-Bitcoin exchange-traded funds for a ninth straight session, the longest run of withdrawals since the products debuted, underscoring a cooling in demand for the largest cryptocurrency even as broader risk assets rally.

title: France’s Economy Unexpectedly Contracted in Early 2026
summary: France’s economy unexpectedly shrank at the start of the year, raising concerns over its resilience to the fallout from the Iran war.

title: Deutsche Bank Hikes Chairman Pay Despite Investor Critique
summary: Deutsche Bank AG’s shareholders approved a plan to give a big pay raise to Supervisory Board Chairman Alexander Wynaendts after criticism at the lender’s annual general meeting.

title: Euro-Zone Consumer Price Expectations May Rise More, ECB Warns
summary: The Iran war may push medium-term inflation expectations among euro-area consumers higher, according to a European Central Bank blog post that appears to underpin the case for hiking interest rates.

title: India Cuts Monsoon Outlook to 90% of Normal in Risk to Crops
summary: India lowered this year’s monsoon forecast, a blow to farmers who are already facing higher input costs due to the Middle East conflict.

title: Blue Origin's New Glenn Rocket Explodes on Launchpad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded while undergoing a test on a launchpad in Florida on Thursday evening. The explosion is the latest blow to its reputation as a reliable alternative to SpaceX’s Falcon 9. (Source: @JConcilus via Associated Press)

title: Hillhouse’s Elham Adds to Asia Private Debt Boom With New Fund
summary: Elham Credit Partners is planning its second Asia Pacific-focused fund, according to people familiar with the matter, underscoring sustained investor demand in the region even as the global $1.8 trillion industry grapples with mounting challenges.

title: Blue Origin Rocket Explodes on Launchpad in Florida
summary: User generated video verified by Associated Press shows the Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket explode in a massive fireball while undergoing a test on a Florida launchpad Thursday evening. The firm was preparing the vehicle for its fourth launch, which was slated to deploy a batch of satellites for Amazon.com Inc.’s Leo, a rival satellite network to SpaceX’s Starlink. (Source: Bloomberg)
